docs/decisions.purpose: Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) documenting why OpenMeter chose its core infrastructure stack (Kafka, CloudEvents, ClickHouse, partitioning, idempotency). These are immutable historical records — the constraints they document are baked into the codebase and must not be reversed without a new numbered ADR.

patterns.name: ADR sequential numbering
patterns.description: Files are named 000N-<slug>.md with zero-padded sequence numbers. Each follows: Context/Problem Statement → Considered Options → Decision Outcome → Consequences.

patterns.name: Decision recorded with tradeoffs
patterns.description: Every ADR documents Pros AND Cons of the chosen option AND rejected alternatives. Omitting Cons makes the ADR incomplete.
patterns.example: 0003-idempotency.md lists Redis key-expiration and bloom-filter alternatives with explicit cons before the ksqlDB windowed-table choice.

patterns.name: Supersession by new ADR
patterns.description: When a later ADR overrides an earlier one (e.g. ClickHouse replacing ksqlDB), the new ADR is the authority; the old ADR is not edited — it remains as historical context.
patterns.example: 0005-clickhouse.md supersedes parts of 0001 and 0003; ClickHouse is now the analytics store, not ksqlDB.

key_file_guides.role: Why Kafka was chosen as the event streaming backbone. The 'keep interfaces generic' decision is why streaming.Connector is an abstraction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The ksqlDB choice here is superseded by 0005-clickhouse.md — do not treat ksqlDB as the current processor.

key_file_guides.role: Why CloudEvents is the ingest wire format; all ingest code in openmeter/ingest/ and openmeter/sink/ works with CloudEvents structs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CloudEvents are unique by ID + Source — this uniqueness contract underlies the deduplication logic in openmeter/dedupe/.

key_file_guides.role: Establishes the 32-day deduplication window and ID+Source uniqueness key, surfacing in openmeter/sink/ and openmeter/dedupe/.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The ksqlDB deduplication described here is superseded by the Redis/in-memory deduplicator; the 32-day window is the enduring constraint.

key_file_guides.role: Why subject is the Kafka partition key and why the default partition count is 100.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Co-partitioning requirements constrain ksqlDB join operations — relevant when adding new Kafka topics.

key_file_guides.role: Documents replacing ksqlDB with ClickHouse + Kafka Connect; the AggregatingMergeTree + MaterializedView pre-aggregation strategy underlies meter queries in openmeter/streaming/clickhouse/.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This ADR supersedes parts of 0001 and 0003 — ClickHouse is now the analytics store, not ksqlDB.

anti_patterns: Editing existing ADR files to change the recorded decision — create a new numbered ADR instead.
Skipping the 'Considered Options' section when writing a new ADR.
Using this folder for operational runbooks or migration guides — those belong in docs/migration-guides.
Omitting the Cons section for the chosen option — every decision must document its tradeoffs.

decisions.decision: CloudEvents as the universal event format.
decisions.rationale: Enables integration with cloud infrastructure, multi-language SDKs, and built-in uniqueness via ID+Source without a custom format.
ksqlDB had scaling limitations for small-to-medium producers and lacked clusterization; ClickHouse with AggregatingMergeTree handles pre-aggregation more efficiently.

docs/migration-guides.purpose: User-facing operator migration guides for breaking changes between OpenMeter versions. Each file covers one breaking change: what changed, SQL/API migration steps, and deprecation timeline. These are operator-facing documents, not developer architecture patterns.

patterns.name: Date-prefixed filename
patterns.description: Files are named YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md where the date is the release date of the breaking change, not the writing date.

patterns.name: SQL migration scripts embedded inline
patterns.description: Guides requiring DB changes embed SQL directly in fenced sql blocks. Read-only SELECT audit queries come first; destructive UPDATE/DELETE statements come after.
patterns.example: 2025-06-26-subscription-alignment.md provides SELECT COUNT(*) audit queries before UPDATE plans SET billables_must_align = true.

patterns.name: Before/After API examples for SDK migrations
patterns.description: API-level changes show TypeScript before/after code blocks using the @openmeter/sdk client so SDK consumers can update integration code directly.

patterns.name: Deprecation timeline table
patterns.description: Guides with phased deprecations include a Markdown table with Date and Change columns documenting when deprecated APIs are removed.

patterns.name: Versioned event deprecation pattern
patterns.description: Event schema breaking changes follow: introduce V(N+1), stop producing VN, drain VN (with an upgrade warning), then remove VN in a later release.
patterns.example: 2025-11-04-entitlement-events-v1.md: V2 introduced in commit 85f7ec90, V1 production stopped, V1 removed after drain confirmed.

key_file_guides.role: Migration from unaligned to aligned subscriptions (billables_must_align flag). References openmeter/productcatalog/errors.go#L411 and alignment.go#L22 for the validation logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SQL targets plans and subscriptions tables directly — the billables_must_align column must still exist for these queries to work.

key_file_guides.role: Consolidation of subject and customer entities — subjects deprecated as primary billing entities in favor of customers; explains automatic usage attribution by customer ID/Key.
key_file_guides.watch_for: usageAttribution.subjects is now optional; code assuming it is required needs updating. Subject APIs targeted for removal November 2025.

key_file_guides.role: Removal notice for V1 entitlement events after V2 replacement. Operators must drain V1 events before upgrading.
key_file_guides.watch_for: When adding new entitlement event versions, follow the same pattern: introduce V(N+1), stop producing VN, drain VN, then remove VN later.

anti_patterns: Writing a migration guide without audit SELECT queries before destructive UPDATE/DELETE statements.
Referencing internal Go file paths without also explaining the user-visible behavior change.
Combining multiple unrelated breaking changes into a single guide file — one breaking change per file.
Using these files as ADRs for architectural decisions — those belong in docs/decisions.

decisions.decision: SQL scripts embedded directly in migration guides rather than as separate .sql files.
decisions.rationale: Keeps migration context (what, why, SQL) together in one document operators read linearly; separate files risk being run out of order or without context.

decisions.decision: Phased deprecation with explicit removal dates.
decisions.rationale: API consumers need advance notice; a two-phase approach (deprecate then remove) gives time to migrate without blocking internal codebase cleanup.
